title: Short Story — Brothers
html:
TL;DR

  • High-school short story (March 2020, Yahir age 15) about Nikolas, a 15-year-old grappling with "Steve Jobsinian" urgency — "why live a 60 year life in 60 years when you can accomplish that in 20" — invoking Tupac and kobe bryant as die-young-leave-everything icons
  • Frame story: Nikolas's grandfather Didi tells him about his Ukrainian brother Sabonis — a 16-year-old who left rural Lviv with him for Warsaw, started at a butcher shop, met patron Antoni Dabrowski (WW1-veteran-turned-art-hedge-fund-owner), branched off into venture capital, struck gold on early Volkswagen stake, died of cardiac arrest at 42
  • The fable's lesson Didi gives: "One can embrace 'it,' or one can run away from 'it,' either way 'it' is coming" — the Yahir-encoded version of memento mori decision filter
  • Anti-resolution ending: Nik is briefly motivated for 2 weeks, then his "quench to strive deteriorates" and he's "stuck in a rut of complacency" — the rut-pattern that recurs throughout Yahir's later journals
  • Earliest vault articulation of the ambition vs brotherhood tension and the burn bright die young ambivalence

Notes

The earliest piece of Yahir-fiction in the vault, written at 15 for what looks like a sophomore-year creative writing assignment. The Didi/Sabonis frame is a transparently autobiographical proxy: two brothers from a hand-to-mouth household, one with relentless ambition (Sabonis ≈ aspirational-Yahir), one who anchors and survives (Didi ≈ steady-Yahir / steady-emilio). The Sabonis arc — Warsaw butcher shop → Antoni's hedge fund → solo VC → early-Volkswagen → cardiac-arrest at 42 — is a teenager's compression of the 10x ambition payoff plus its mortality cost.

Cultural index of 15-year-old Yahir: Gary Vee entrepreneur-TikTok, Steve Jobs, Tupac, kobe bryant — the die-young-having-done-everything canon. The framing that Gary Vee is "maybe bullshit content that aims to ease the unemployed-overweight 26 year old behind the screen" is unusually skeptical for the era, and prefigures Yahir's adult skepticism of -as-cope.

The end where Nik's motivation lasts 2 weeks then dissolves is the rut of complacency pattern — recurs across 2022 01 07 failure is terrifying and humiliating it means not fittting, 2022 10 15 things on 10 1415 2022 (Dad's rut), and the 60 day reframe later.

related.label: Failure as not-fitting-in — the bass-thump-behind-you frequency theory
